baseline: Walks slowly with a walker and prefers staff to approach from the front.
Usually eats best in a quiet seat away from corridor traffic.
communication_cues: Use short, calm sentences and allow extra time before repeating a question.
Responds well when staff explain each care step before touching equipment.
preferences: Prefers the window-side chair for afternoon rest.
known_triggers: Corridor noise during evening medication rounds.
Unfamiliar staff entering quickly without greeting.
calming_approaches: Lower voice volume, reduce background noise, and offer a two-minute pause.
Invite family-context conversation before retrying routine care.
family_context_notes: Daughter usually visits on Wednesday afternoons.
Family reports music from older piano lessons can help during anxious moments.
recent_history: Several notes mention slower walking after lunch.
Medication refusal has appeared more than once when corridor noise is high.
watch_patterns: Repeated medication refusal language with unfamiliar staff.
